quiz.title: Unit 10 Quiz 5, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Mole Fraction, Volume from Molarity, and Percent by Mass
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Mole Fraction Calculations, Volume from Molarity, Percent by Mass, Solution Concentration

questions.question: What is the mole fraction of sucrose (C₁₂H₂₂O₁₁) in a solution labeled as 25% sucrose by mass?
questions.explanation:
Assume 100 g solution: 25 g sucrose, 75 g water
Molar mass sucrose = 342.30 g/mol, water = 18.02 g/mol
Moles sucrose = 25/342.30 = 0.0730 mol
Moles water = 75/18.02 = 4.16 mol
Mole fraction sucrose = 0.0730/(0.0730+4.16) = 0.0173

questions.question: Calculate the mole fraction of glycerol (C₃H₈O₃) in a solution containing 30 g of glycerol and 70 g of water.
questions.explanation:
Molar mass glycerol = 92.09 g/mol
Moles glycerol = 30/92.09 = 0.326 mol
Molar mass water = 18.02 g/mol
Moles water = 70/18.02 = 3.88 mol
Mole fraction glycerol = 0.326/(0.326+3.88) = 0.0775

questions.question: A solution is prepared by dissolving 12 g of urea (NH₂CONH₂) in 180 g of water. Find the mole fraction of urea.
questions.explanation:
Molar mass urea = 60.06 g/mol
Moles urea = 12/60.06 = 0.200 mol
Molar mass water = 18.02 g/mol
Moles water = 180/18.02 = 9.99 mol
Mole fraction urea = 0.200/(0.200+9.99) = 0.0196

questions.question: Calculate the mole fraction of benzene (C₆H₆) in a solution containing 30% benzene by mass in toluene (C₇H₈).
questions.explanation:
Assume 100 g solution: 30 g benzene, 70 g toluene
Molar mass benzene = 78.11 g/mol
Moles benzene = 30/78.11 = 0.384 mol
Molar mass toluene = 92.14 g/mol
Moles toluene = 70/92.14 = 0.760 mol
Mole fraction benzene = 0.384/(0.384+0.760) = 0.336

questions.question: What is the mole fraction of H₂O₂ in a 'drugstore' solution that is 3% H₂O₂ by mass?
questions.explanation:
Assume 100 g solution: 3 g H₂O₂, 97 g water
Molar mass H₂O₂ = 34.02 g/mol
Moles H₂O₂ = 3/34.02 = 0.0882 mol
Molar mass water = 18.02 g/mol
Moles water = 97/18.02 = 5.38 mol
Mole fraction H₂O₂ = 0.0882/(0.0882+5.38) = 0.0161

questions.question: Calculate the mole fraction of acetic acid (CH₃COOH) in a vinegar solution that is 5% acetic acid by mass.
questions.explanation:
Assume 100 g solution: 5 g acetic acid, 95 g water
Molar mass acetic acid = 60.05 g/mol
Moles acetic acid = 5/60.05 = 0.0833 mol
Molar mass water = 18.02 g/mol
Moles water = 95/18.02 = 5.27 mol
Mole fraction acetic acid = 0.0833/(0.0833+5.27) = 0.0156

questions.question: Calculate the volume of a 0.75 M aqueous solution made from 1.5 moles of KNO₃.
questions.explanation: Volume (L) = moles / molarity = 1.5 mol / 0.75 M = 2.0 L

questions.question: Calculate the volume of a 1.25 M aqueous solution made from 5.0 moles of CuSO₄.
Calculate the volume of a 0.20 M aqueous solution made from 0.050 moles of AgNO₃.
Calculate the volume of a 3.5 M aqueous solution made from 14 moles of LiCl.
Calculate the volume of a 0.050 M aqueous solution made from 0.010 moles of Ca(OH)₂.
Calculate the mass percent of 40 g of sucrose in a solution that has a total mass of 200 g.
Calculate the percent by mass of 12 g of AgNO₃ dissolved in 150 g of water.
questions.explanation:
Total mass = 12 g + 150 g = 162 g
Percent by mass = (12/162) × 100% = 7.41%

questions.question: 35 g of CaCl₂ is dissolved in 125 g of water. What is the percent by mass of the solute?
questions.explanation:
Total mass = 35 g + 125 g = 160 g
Percent by mass = (35/160) × 100% = 21.9%

questions.question: Calculate the percent by mass of 22.5 g of KBr in 177.5 g of water.
questions.explanation:
Total mass = 22.5 g + 177.5 g = 200 g
Percent by mass = (22.5/200) × 100% = 11.25%

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered mole fractions, volume calculations, and percent by mass! 🧪📊
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the formulas for mole fraction and volume from molarity.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on calculating moles correctly and using the right formulas.
scoreMessages.0: Please review mole fraction calculations (χ = n₁/n total), volume from molarity (V = n/M), and percent by mass.
